NKJV.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O LORD, You have searched me and known me.
NKJV.2: You know my sitting down and my rising up; You understand my thought afar off.
NKJV.3: You comprehend my path and my lying down, And are acquainted with all my ways.
NKJV.4: For there is not a word on my tongue, But behold, O LORD, You know it altogether.
NKJV.5: You have hedged me behind and before, And laid Your hand upon me.
NKJV.6: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; It is high, I cannot attain it.
NKJV.7: Where can I go from Your Spirit? Or where can I flee from Your presence?
NKJV.8: If I ascend into heaven, You are there; If I make my bed in hell, behold, You are there.
NKJV.9: If I take the wings of the morning, And dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
NKJV.10: Even there Your hand shall lead me, And Your right hand shall hold me.
NKJV.11: If I say, “Surely the darkness shall fall on me,” Even the night shall be light about me;
NKJV.12: Indeed, the darkness shall not hide from You, But the night shines as the day; The darkness and the light are both alike to You.
NKJV.13: For You formed my inward parts; You covered me in my mother’s womb.
NKJV.14: I will praise You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; Marvelous are Your works, And that my soul knows very well.
NKJV.15: My frame was not hidden from You, When I was made in secret, And skillfully wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.
NKJV.16: Your eyes saw my substance, being yet unformed. And in Your book they all were written, The days fashioned for me, When as yet there were none of them.
NKJV.17: How precious also are Your thoughts to me, O God! How great is the sum of them!
NKJV.18: If I should count them, they would be more in number than the sand; When I awake, I am still with You.
NKJV.19: Oh, that You would slay the wicked, O God!  Depart from me, therefore, you bloodthirsty men.
NKJV.20: For they speak against You wickedly; Your enemies take Your name in vain.
NKJV.21: Do I not hate them, O LORD, who hate You? And do I not loathe those who rise up against You?
NKJV.22: I hate them with perfect hatred; I count them my enemies.
NKJV.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; Try me, and know my anxieties;
NKJV.24: And see if there is any wicked way in me, And lead me in the way everlasting.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S>, thou hast searched<S>2713</S> me, and known<S>3045</S> me.

KJV.3: Thou compassest<S>2219</S> my path<S>734</S> and my lying down<S>7252</S>, and art acquainted<S>5532</S> with all my ways<S>1870</S>. <sup>compassest: or, winnowest</sup>

KJV.6: Such knowledge<S>1847</S> is too wonderful<S>6383</S> <S>6383</S> for me; it is high<S>7682</S>, I cannot<S>3201</S> attain unto it.
KJV.7: Whither shall I go<S>3212</S> from thy spirit<S>7307</S>? or whither shall I flee<S>1272</S> from thy presence<S>6440</S>?

KJV.12: Yea, the darkness<S>2822</S> hideth<S>2821</S> not from thee; but the night<S>3915</S> shineth<S>215</S> as the day<S>3117</S>: the darkness<S>2825</S> and the light<S>219</S> are both alike to thee. <sup>hideth: Heb. darkeneth not</sup> <sup>the darkness and: Heb. as is the darkness, so is the light</sup>
KJV.13: For thou hast possessed<S>7069</S> my reins<S>3629</S>: thou hast covered<S>5526</S> me in my mother's<S>517</S> womb<S>990</S>.
KJV.14: I will praise<S>3034</S> thee; for I am fearfully<S>3372</S> and wonderfully made<S>6395</S>: marvellous<S>6381</S> are thy works<S>4639</S>; and that my soul<S>5315</S> knoweth<S>3045</S> right well<S>3966</S>. <sup>right: Heb. greatly</sup>
KJV.15: My substance<S>6108</S> was not hid<S>3582</S> from thee, when I was made<S>6213</S> in secret<S>5643</S>, and curiously wrought<S>7551</S> in the lowest parts<S>8482</S> of the earth<S>776</S>. <sup>substance: or, strength, or, body</sup>
KJV.16: Thine eyes<S>5869</S> did see<S>7200</S> my substance, yet being unperfect<S>1564</S>; and in thy book<S>5612</S> all my members were written<S>3789</S>, which in continuance<S>3117</S> were fashioned<S>3335</S>, when as yet there was none<S>259</S> of them. <sup>all: Heb. all of them</sup> <sup>which: or, what days they should be fashioned</sup>
KJV.17: How precious<S>3365</S> also are thy thoughts<S>7454</S> unto me, O God<S>410</S>! how great<S>6105</S> is the sum<S>7218</S> of them!

KJV.22: I hate<S>8130</S> them with perfect<S>8503</S> hatred<S>8135</S>: I count them mine enemies<S>341</S>.

KJV.24: And see<S>7200</S> if there be any wicked<S>6090</S> way<S>1870</S> in me, and lead<S>5148</S> me in the way<S>1870</S> everlasting<S>5769</S>. <sup>wicked: Heb. way of pain, or, grief</sup>

ESV.1: O LORD, you have searched me and known me!
ESV.2: You know when I sit down and when I rise up; you discern my thoughts from afar.
ESV.3: You search out my path and my lying down and are acquainted with all my ways.
ESV.4: Even before a word is on my tongue, behold, O LORD, you know it altogether.
ESV.5: You hem me in, behind and before, and lay your hand upon me.
ESV.6: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high; I cannot attain it.
ESV.7: Where shall I go from your Spirit? Or where shall I flee from your presence?
ESV.8: If I ascend to heaven, you are there! If I make my bed in Sheol, you are there!
ESV.9: If I take the wings of the morning and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
ESV.10: even there your hand shall lead me, and your right hand shall hold me.
ESV.11: If I say, “Surely the darkness shall cover me, and the light about me be night,”
ESV.12: even the darkness is not dark to you; the night is bright as the day, for darkness is as light with you.
ESV.13: For you formed my inward parts; you knitted me together in my mother's womb.
ESV.14: I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.  Wonderful are your works; my soul knows it very well.
ESV.15: My frame was not hidden from you, when I was being made in secret, intricately woven in the depths of the earth.
ESV.16: Your eyes saw my unformed substance; in your book were written, every one of them, the days that were formed for me, when as yet there was none of them.
ESV.17: How precious to me are your thoughts, O God! How vast is the sum of them!
ESV.18: If I would count them, they are more than the sand. I awake, and I am still with you.
ESV.19: Oh that you would slay the wicked, O God! O men of blood, depart from me!
ESV.20: They speak against you with malicious intent; your enemies take your name in vain.
ESV.21: Do I not hate those who hate you, O LORD? And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
ESV.22: I hate them with complete hatred; I count them my enemies.
ESV.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart! Try me and know my thoughts!
ESV.24: And see if there be any grievous way in me, and lead me in the way everlasting!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. O LORD, You have searched me and known [me].
NASB.2: You know when I sit down and when I rise up; You understand my thought from afar.
NASB.3: You scrutinize my path and my lying down, And are intimately acquainted with all my ways.
NASB.4: Even before there is a word on my tongue, Behold, O LORD, You know it all.
NASB.5: You have enclosed me behind and before, And laid Your hand upon me.
NASB.6: [Such] knowledge is too wonderful for me; It is [too] high, I cannot attain to it.
NASB.7: Where can I go from Your Spirit? Or where can I flee from Your presence?
NASB.8: If I ascend to heaven, You are there; If I make my bed in Sheol, behold, You are there.
NASB.9: If I take the wings of the dawn, If I dwell in the remotest part of the sea,
NASB.10: Even there Your hand will lead me, And Your right hand will lay hold of me.
NASB.11: If I say, 'Surely the darkness will overwhelm me, And the light around me will be night,'
NASB.12: Even the darkness is not dark to You, And the night is as bright as the day. Darkness and light are alike [to You].
NASB.13: For You formed my inward parts; You wove me in my mother's womb.
NASB.14: I will give thanks to You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; Wonderful are Your works, And my soul knows it very well.
NASB.15: My frame was not hidden from You, When I was made in secret, [And] skillfully wrought in the depths of the earth;
NASB.16: Your eyes have seen my unformed substance; And in Your book were all written The days that were ordained [for me], When as yet there was not one of them.
NASB.17: How precious also are Your thoughts to me, O God! How vast is the sum of them!
NASB.18: If I should count them, they would outnumber the sand. When I awake, I am still with You.
NASB.19: O that You would slay the wicked, O God; Depart from me, therefore, men of bloodshed.
NASB.20: For they speak against You wickedly, And Your enemies take [Your name] in vain.
NASB.21: Do I not hate those who hate You, O LORD? And do I not loathe those who rise up against You?
NASB.22: I hate them with the utmost hatred; They have become my enemies.
NASB.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; Try me and know my anxious thoughts;
NASB.24: And see if there be any hurtful way in me, And lead me in the everlasting way.

NLT.1:
O LORD, you have examined my heart
and know everything about me.
NLT.2:
You know when I sit down or stand up.
You know my thoughts even when I’m far away.
NLT.3:
You see me when I travel
and when I rest at home.
You know everything I do.
NLT.4:
You know what I am going to say
even before I say it, LORD.
NLT.5:
You go before me and follow me.
You place your hand of blessing on my head.
NLT.6:
Such knowledge is too wonderful for me,
too great for me to understand!
NLT.7:
I can never escape from your Spirit!
I can never get away from your presence!
NLT.8:
If I go up to heaven, you are there;
if I go down to the grave, you are there.
NLT.9:
If I ride the wings of the morning,
if I dwell by the farthest oceans,
NLT.10:
even there your hand will guide me,
and your strength will support me.
NLT.11:
I could ask the darkness to hide me
and the light around me to become night —
NLT.12:
but even in darkness I cannot hide from you.
To you the night shines as bright as day.
Darkness and light are the same to you.
NLT.13:
You made all the delicate, inner parts of my body
and knit me together in my mother’s womb.
NLT.14:
Thank you for making me so wonderfully complex!
Your workmanship is marvelous — how well I know it.
NLT.15:
You watched me as I was being formed in utter seclusion,
as I was woven together in the dark of the womb.
NLT.16:
You saw me before I was born.
Every day of my life was recorded in your book.
Every moment was laid out
before a single day had passed.
NLT.17:
How precious are your thoughts about me, O God.
They cannot be numbered!
NLT.18:
I can’t even count them;
they outnumber the grains of sand!
And when I wake up,
you are still with me!
NLT.19:
O God, if only you would destroy the wicked!
Get out of my life, you murderers!
NLT.20:
They blaspheme you;
your enemies misuse your name.
NLT.21:
O LORD, shouldn’t I hate those who hate you?
Shouldn’t I despise those who oppose you?
NLT.22:
Yes, I hate them with total hatred,
for your enemies are my enemies.
NLT.23:
Search me, O God, and know my heart;
test me and know my anxious thoughts.
NLT.24:
Point out anything in me that offends you,
and lead me along the path of everlasting life.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Yahweh, you have searched me, and you know me.
WEB.2: You know my sitting down and my rising up. You perceive my thoughts from afar.
WEB.3: You search out my path and my lying down, and are acquainted with all my ways.
WEB.4: For there is not a word on my tongue, but, behold, Yahweh, you know it altogether.
WEB.5: You hem me in behind and before. You laid your hand on me.
WEB.6: This knowledge is beyond me. It’s lofty. I can’t attain it.
WEB.7: Where could I go from your Spirit? Or where could I flee from your presence?
WEB.8: If I ascend up into heaven, you are there. If I make my bed in Sheol,  behold, you are there!
WEB.9: If I take the wings of the dawn, and settle in the uttermost parts of the sea;
WEB.10: Even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me.
WEB.11: If I say, “Surely the darkness will overwhelm me; the light around me will be night”;
WEB.12: even the darkness doesn’t hide from you, but the night shines as the day. The darkness is like light to you.
WEB.13: For you formed my inmost being. You knit me together in my mother’s womb.
WEB.14: I will give thanks to you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful. My soul knows that very well.
WEB.15: My frame wasn’t hidden from you, when I was made in secret, woven together in the depths of the earth.
WEB.16: Your eyes saw my body. In your book they were all written, the days that were ordained for me, when as yet there were none of them.
WEB.17: How precious to me are your thoughts, God! How vast is their sum!
WEB.18: If I would count them, they are more in number than the sand. When I wake up, I am still with you.
WEB.19: If only you, God, would kill the wicked. Get away from me, you bloodthirsty men!
WEB.20: For they speak against you wickedly. Your enemies take your name in vain.
WEB.21: Yahweh, don’t I hate those who hate you? Am I not grieved with those who rise up against you?
WEB.22: I hate them with perfect hatred. They have become my enemies.
WEB.23: Search me, God, and know my heart. Try me, and know my thoughts.
WEB.24: See if there is any wicked way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

CSB17.1: For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] LORD, you have searched me and known me. ⓐ
CSB17.2:
You know when I sit down and when I stand up;
you understand my thoughts from far away. ⓑ
CSB17.3:
You observe my travels and my rest;
you are aware of all my ways. ⓒ
CSB17.4:
Before a word is on my tongue,
you know all about it, LORD. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
You have encircled me;
you have placed your hand on me. ⓔ
CSB17.6:
This wondrous knowledge is beyond me.
It is lofty; I am unable to reach it. ⓕ
CSB17.7:
Where can I go to escape your Spirit?
Where can I flee from your presence? ⓖ
CSB17.8:
If I go up to heaven, you are there; ⓗ
if I make my bed in Sheol, you are there. ⓘ
CSB17.9:
If I live at the eastern horizon
or settle at the western limits, [1]
CSB17.10:
even there your hand will lead me;
your right hand will hold on to me. ⓙ
CSB17.11:
If I say, “Surely the darkness will hide me,
and the light around me will be night” ⓚ  —
CSB17.12:
even the darkness is not dark to you.
The night shines like the day;
darkness and light are alike to you. ⓛ
CSB17.13:
For it was you who created my inward parts; [2]
you knit me together in my mother’s womb. ⓜ
CSB17.14:
I will praise you
because I have been remarkably and wondrously made. [3] [4]
Your works are wondrous,
and I know this very well. ⓝ
CSB17.15:
My bones were not hidden from you
when I was made in secret,
when I was formed in the depths of the earth. ⓐ
CSB17.16:
Your eyes saw me when I was formless;
all my days were written in your book and planned
before a single one of them began. ⓑ
CSB17.17:
God, how precious [5] your thoughts are to me;
how vast their sum is!
CSB17.18:
If I counted them,
they would outnumber the grains of sand; ⓒ
when I wake up, [6] I am still with you. ⓓ
CSB17.19:
God, if only you would kill the wicked —
you bloodthirsty men, stay away from me ⓔ  —
CSB17.20:
who invoke you deceitfully.
Your enemies swear by you falsely. ⓕ
CSB17.21:
LORD, don’t I hate those who hate you,
and detest those who rebel against you?
CSB17.22:
I hate them with extreme hatred;
I consider them my enemies. ⓖ
CSB17.23:
Search me, God, and know my heart;
test me and know my concerns. ⓗ
CSB17.24:
See if there is any offensive [7] way in me;
lead me in the everlasting way. ⓘ

AMP.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O LORD, you have searched me [thoroughly] and have known me.
AMP.2:
You know when I sit down and when I rise up [my entire life, everything I do];
You understand my thought from afar.
AMP.3:
You scrutinize my path and my lying down,
And You are intimately acquainted with all my ways.
AMP.4:
Even before there is a word on my tongue [still unspoken],
Behold, O LORD, You know it all.
AMP.5:
You have enclosed me behind and before,
And [You have] placed Your hand upon me.
AMP.6:
Such [infinite] knowledge is too wonderful for me;
It is too high [above me], I cannot reach it.
AMP.7:
Where can I go from Your Spirit?
Or where can I flee from Your presence?
AMP.8:
If I ascend to heaven, You are there;
If I make my bed in Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead), behold, You are there.
AMP.9:
If I take the wings of the dawn,
If I dwell in the remotest part of the sea,
AMP.10:
Even there Your hand will lead me,
And Your right hand will take hold of me.
AMP.11:
If I say, "Surely the darkness will cover me,
And the night will be the only light around me,"
AMP.12:
Even the darkness is not dark to You and conceals nothing from You,
But the night shines as bright as the day;
Darkness and light are alike to You.
AMP.13:
For You formed my innermost parts;
You knit me [together] in my mother's womb.
AMP.14:
I will give thanks and praise to You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made;
Wonderful are Your works,
And my soul knows it very well.
AMP.15:
My frame was not hidden from You,
When I was being formed in secret,
And intricately and skillfully formed [as if embroidered with many colors] in the depths of the earth.
AMP.16:
Your eyes have seen my unformed substance;
And in Your book were all written
The days that were appointed for me,
When as yet there was not one of them [even taking shape].
AMP.17:
How precious also are Your thoughts to me, O God!
How vast is the sum of them!
AMP.18:
If I could count them, they would outnumber the sand.
When I awake, I am still with You.
AMP.19:
O that You would kill the wicked, O God;
Go away from me, therefore, men of bloodshed.
AMP.20:
For they speak against You wickedly,
Your enemies take Your name in vain.
AMP.21:
Do I not hate those who hate You, O LORD?
And do I not loathe those who rise up against You?
AMP.22:
I hate them with perfect and utmost hatred;
They have become my enemies.
AMP.23:
Search me [thoroughly], O God, and know my heart;
Test me and know my anxious thoughts;
AMP.24:
And see if there is any wicked or hurtful way in me,
And lead me in the everlasting way.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, investigate my life;
get all the facts firsthand.
MSG.2:
I'm an open book to you;
even from a distance, you know what I'm thinking.
MSG.3:
You know when I leave and when I get back;
I'm never out of your sight.
MSG.4:
You know everything I'm going to say
before I start the first sentence.
MSG.5:
I look behind me and you're there,
then up ahead and you're there, too—
your reassuring presence, coming and going.
MSG.6:
This is too much, too wonderful—
I can't take it all in!
MSG.7:
Is there anyplace I can go to avoid your Spirit?
to be out of your sight?
MSG.8:
If I climb to the sky, you're there!
If I go underground, you're there!
MSG.9:
If I flew on morning's wings
to the far western horizon,
MSG.10:
You'd find me in a minute—
you're already there waiting!
MSG.11:
Then I said to myself, "Oh, he even sees me in the dark!
At night I'm immersed in the light!"
MSG.12:
It's a fact: darkness isn't dark to you;
night and day, darkness and light, they're all the same to you.
MSG.13:
Oh yes, you shaped me first inside, then out;
you formed me in my mother's womb.
MSG.14:
I thank you, High God—you're breathtaking!
Body and soul, I am marvelously made!
I worship in adoration—what a creation!
MSG.15:
You know me inside and out,
you know every bone in my body;
You know exactly how I was made, bit by bit,
how I was sculpted from nothing into something.
MSG.16:
Like an open book, you watched me grow from conception to birth;
all the stages of my life were spread out before you,
The days of my life all prepared
before I'd even lived one day.
MSG.17:
Your thoughts—how rare, how beautiful!
God, I'll never comprehend them!
MSG.18:
I couldn't even begin to count them—
any more than I could count the sand of the sea.
Oh, let me rise in the morning and live always with you!
MSG.19:
And please, God, do away with wickedness for good!
And you murderers—out of here!—
MSG.20:
all the men and women who belittle you, God,
infatuated with cheap god-imitations.
MSG.21:
See how I hate those who hate you, God,
see how I loathe all this godless arrogance;
MSG.22:
I hate it with pure, unadulterated hatred.
Your enemies are my enemies!
MSG.23:
Investigate my life, O God,
find out everything about me;
Cross-examine and test me,
get a clear picture of what I'm about;
MSG.24:
See for yourself whether I've done anything wrong—
then guide me on the road to eternal life.

NIV.1:
Psalm 139
For the director of music. Of David. A psalm.
O Lord, you have searched me
and you know me.
NIV.2:
You know when I sit and when I rise;
you perceive my thoughts from afar.
NIV.3:
You discern my going out and my lying down;
you are familiar with all my ways.
NIV.4:
Before a word is on my tongue
you know it completely, O Lord.
NIV.5:
You hem me in—behind and before;
you have laid your hand upon me.
NIV.6:
Such knowledge is too wonderful for me,
too lofty for me to attain.
NIV.7:
Where can I go from your Spirit?
Where can I flee from your presence?
NIV.8:
If I go up to the heavens, you are there;
if I make my bed in the depths, you are there.
NIV.9:
If I rise on the wings of the dawn,
if I settle on the far side of the sea,
NIV.10:
even there your hand will guide me,
your right hand will hold me fast.
NIV.11:
If I say, “Surely the darkness will hide me
and the light become night around me,”
NIV.12:
even the darkness will not be dark to you;
the night will shine like the day,
for darkness is as light to you.
NIV.13:
For you created my inmost being;
you knit me together in my mother’s womb.
NIV.14:
I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made;
your works are wonderful,
I know that full well.
NIV.15:
My frame was not hidden from you
when I was made in the secret place.
When I was woven together in the depths of the earth,
NIV.16:
your eyes saw my unformed body.
All the days ordained for me
were written in your book
before one of them came to be.
NIV.17:
How precious to me are your thoughts, O God!
How vast is the sum of them!
NIV.18:
Were I to count them,
they would outnumber the grains of sand.
When I awake,
I am still with you.
NIV.19:
If only you would slay the wicked, O God!
Away from me, you bloodthirsty men!
NIV.20:
They speak of you with evil intent;
your adversaries misuse your name.
NIV.21:
Do I not hate those who hate you, O Lord,
and abhor those who rise up against you?
NIV.22:
I have nothing but hatred for them;
I count them my enemies.
NIV.23:
Search me, O God, and know my heart;
test me and know my anxious thoughts.
NIV.24:
See if there is any offensive way in me,
and lead me in the way everlasting.

NRSVCE.1: O LORD, you have searched me and known me.
NRSVCE.2:
You know when I sit down and when I rise up;
you discern my thoughts from far away.
NRSVCE.3:
You search out my path and my lying down,
and are acquainted with all my ways.
NRSVCE.4:
Even before a word is on my tongue,
O LORD, you know it completely.
NRSVCE.5:
You hem me in, behind and before,
and lay your hand upon me.
NRSVCE.6:
Such knowledge is too wonderful for me;
it is so high that I cannot attain it.
NRSVCE.7:
Where can I go from your spirit?
Or where can I flee from your presence?
NRSVCE.8:
If I ascend to heaven, you are there;
if I make my bed in Sheol, you are there.
NRSVCE.9:
If I take the wings of the morning
and settle at the farthest limits of the sea,
NRSVCE.10:
even there your hand shall lead me,
and your right hand shall hold me fast.
NRSVCE.11:
If I say, “Surely the darkness shall cover me,
and the light around me become night,”
NRSVCE.12:
even the darkness is not dark to you;
the night is as bright as the day,
for darkness is as light to you.
NRSVCE.13:
For it was you who formed my inward parts;
you knit me together in my mother’s womb.
NRSVCE.14:
I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.
Wonderful are your works;
that I know very well.
NRSVCE.15:
My frame was not hidden from you,
when I was being made in secret,
intricately woven in the depths of the earth.
NRSVCE.16:
Your eyes beheld my unformed substance.
In your book were written
all the days that were formed for me,
when none of them as yet existed.
NRSVCE.17:
How weighty to me are your thoughts, O God!
How vast is the sum of them!
NRSVCE.18:
I try to count them — they are more than the sand;
I come to the end — I am still with you.
NRSVCE.19:
O that you would kill the wicked, O God,
and that the bloodthirsty would depart from me —
NRSVCE.20:
those who speak of you maliciously,
and lift themselves up against you for evil!
NRSVCE.21:
Do I not hate those who hate you, O LORD?
And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
NRSVCE.22:
I hate them with perfect hatred;
I count them my enemies.
NRSVCE.23:
Search me, O God, and know my heart;
test me and know my thoughts.
NRSVCE.24:
See if there is any wicked way in me,
and lead me in the way everlasting.

NET.1: O LORD, you examine me and know.
NET.2: You know when I sit down and when I get up; even from far away you understand my motives.
NET.3: You carefully observe me when I travel or when I lie down to rest; you are aware of everything I do.
NET.4: Certainly my tongue does not frame a word without you, O LORD, being thoroughly aware of it.
NET.5: You squeeze me in from behind and in front; you place your hand on me.
NET.6: Your knowledge is beyond my comprehension; it is so far beyond me, I am unable to fathom it.
NET.7: Where can I go to escape your spirit? Where can I flee to escape your presence?
NET.8: If I were to ascend to heaven, you would be there. If I were to sprawl out in Sheol, there you would be.
NET.9: If I were to fly away on the wings of the dawn, and settle down on the other side of the sea,
NET.10: even there your hand would guide me, your right hand would grab hold of me.
NET.11: If I were to say, “Certainly the darkness will cover me, and the light will turn to night all around me,”
NET.12: even the darkness is not too dark for you to see, and the night is as bright as day; darkness and light are the same to you.
NET.13: Certainly you made my mind and heart; you wove me together in my mother’s womb.
NET.14: I will give you thanks because your deeds are awesome and amazing. You knew me thoroughly;
NET.15: my bones were not hidden from you, when I was made in secret and sewed together in the depths of the earth.
NET.16: Your eyes saw me when I was inside the womb. All the days ordained for me were recorded in your scroll before one of them came into existence.
NET.17: How difficult it is for me to fathom your thoughts about me, O God! How vast is their sum total!
NET.18: If I tried to count them, they would outnumber the grains of sand. Even if I finished counting them, I would still have to contend with you.
NET.19: If only you would kill the wicked, O God! Get away from me, you violent men!
NET.20: They rebel against you and act deceitfully; your enemies lie.
NET.21: O LORD, do I not hate those who hate you, and despise those who oppose you?
NET.22: I absolutely hate them, they have become my enemies!
NET.23: Examine me, and probe my thoughts! Test me, and know my concerns!
NET.24: See if there is any idolatrous tendency in me, and lead me in the reliable ancient path!
